milestones.name: Documentation Foundation
milestones.primary_outcome: The repository contains the engineering standards, architecture foundation, and reusable development rules.

milestones.name: Design System Core
milestones.primary_outcome: A runnable Blazor shell on .NET 10 that renders the design system documentation page. The base primitive components exist and are used by the documentation page.

milestones.name: Architecture Refinement and Project Intelligence
milestones.primary_outcome: Strengthen the long-term engineering foundation: introduce VISION.md, the engineering backlog, structured state, decision log, capability mapping, dashboard definition, self-awareness state, and the dogfooding evolution. Validate the documentation architecture.

milestones.name: Application Shell and Navigation
milestones.primary_outcome: The shell has a sidebar, a top bar, and a content panel. The platform is navigable; every navigation target renders an empty state that uses the design system.

M2.1.title: Application Shell Foundation
M2.1.summary: Two layouts (AppLayout, AppEmptyLayout), two placeholder shell components (AppSidebarSlot, AppTopBarSlot), one presentational helper (AppShellRegion). The M1.1 chrome is retired. 25 new bUnit tests ship; 102 total component tests pass.

M2.2.title: Navigation Registry and Sidebar
M2.2.summary: INavigationRegistry, RouteMetadata, RouteMetadataAttribute, RouteRegistry in src/AiEng.Platform.Application/Navigation/; ServiceCollectionExtensions + NavigationServiceCollectionExtensions in src/AiEng.Platform.App/Composition/; AppSidebar, AppSidebarItem, AppNavItem in src/AiEng.Platform.App/Components/Navigation/; [RouteMetadata] applied to all 6 pages; AppSidebarSlot and AppSidebarSlotTests deleted; AppLayout wired to AppSidebar; Pages_AreReachable_Through_Registry architecture test active and green. 28 new bUnit/integration tests + 1 new architecture test added.
M2.2.commit_message: feat(m2.2): add navigation registry and sidebar

M2.3.title: Top Bar, Breadcrumbs, and Page Headers
M2.3.summary: AppTopBar (replaces AppTopBarSlot), AppThemeToggle (relocated from M1.1 chrome), AppUserAvatarSlot, AppBreadcrumb in src/AiEng.Platform.App/Components/. AppBreadcrumb wired into AppPageHeader.Breadcrumbs on DesignSystem.razor. AppTopBarSlot and AppTopBarSlotTests deleted; AppLayout wired to AppTopBar. 27 new bUnit tests across 4 test files; 6 obsolete AppTopBarSlotTests removed; 146 total component tests pass. Two documented deviations: (1) AppTopBar uses div.app-topbar + Leading/Trailing slots rather than AppStack + AppPageHeader; the surface still composes AppTopBar + AppPageHeader + AppBreadcrumb, matching the plan's intent. (2) Optional architecture test Breadcrumb_Follows_Registry_Parent_Chain was skipped per plan § 8 step 11 which marked it optional.
M2.3.commit_message: feat(m2.3): add top bar, breadcrumb, and page header integration

M2.4.title: Project Intelligence Dashboard
M2.4.summary: IProjectIntelligenceReader + ProjectIntelligenceSnapshot + ProjectIntelligenceReader in src/AiEng.Platform.Application/ProjectIntelligence/; ProjectIntelligenceServiceCollectionExtensions in src/AiEng.Platform.App/Composition/; Dashboard.razor + Dashboard.razor.css in src/AiEng.Platform.App/Components/Pages/; theme toggle bug fixed (added appTheme.current JS function, IsDark updated synchronously before JSInterop, JSDisconnectedException handled); 6 unit + 13 bUnit + 2 architecture tests added (175 total passing). Architecture test Pages_Resolve_State_Through_Reader confirms the Dashboard consumes state only through IProjectIntelligenceReader.

M2.5.title: Empty Routes, Responsive, and Accessibility
M2.5.summary: Five sub-deliverables: (1) Empty routes — Home.razor and NotFound.razor rewritten to use AppCard + AppEmptyState with links to /dashboard and /design-system. (2) Responsive matrix — AppLayout.razor.css has @media rules for the lg / md / sm breakpoints (14rem / 10rem / 8rem sidebar widths); content area gets overflow-y: auto; topbar remains horizontal; docs/ui-principles.md § 10.1 documents the matrix. (3) A11y audit — KeyboardSmokeTests (4 tests), AriaCurrentInvariantTests (5 tests covering breadcrumb aria-current, NavLink active state, sidebar active link), AxeCoreAuditTests (3 tests registered but skipped per ADR-016 / M4-D). (4) T-017 theme toggle fix — @rendermode InteractiveServer added to AppThemeToggle.razor (not to AppLayout.razor; the layout's @Body is a RenderFragment delegate, which Blazor refuses to serialize across the SSR → interactive boundary); the new AppLayout_ThemeToggleWiringTests bUnit test renders the AppLayout, finds the toggle inside the topbar, clicks it, and asserts appTheme.set is invoked. (5) Project-continuity state + implementation report + per-session handoff. 18 new component tests + 3 new architecture tests (skipped) added. Total: 197 passing + 7 skipped, 0 failed (6 unit + 185 bUnit + 6 active architecture + 7 registered-but-disabled).
M2.5.commit_message: feat(m2.5): add empty routes, responsive matrix, a11y audit, and theme toggle fix

M2.6.title: M2 Closeout and Treehouse Dogfooding
M2.6.summary: M2 closeout slice. Introduces the Milestone Closeout Standard at .ai/workflows/milestone-closeout.md (the canonical procedure every future milestone closeout must follow; the 13-section retrospective is the standard's required deliverable). Ships the M2 retrospective at retrospective-m2-application-shell-and-navigation.md (the first milestone retrospective in the repository). Validates the complete M2 milestone end-to-end: npm run css:build (0), dotnet restore (0), dotnet build (0 warnings, 0 errors), dotnet test (197 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped per ADR-016), dotnet format --verify-no-changes (clean), visual smoke (5 routes return 200, theme toggle present on every AppLayout page). Closes M2 (status Done; closed_at 2026-07-11). Updates ROADMAP.md § 2 (M2 row Done) and § 3 (M2.6 row Delivered; M2 DoD expanded). Updates the master delivery plan § 1 (M2 row Done) and § 3 (M2 completion status Done; M2.6 slice row Delivered). Prepares the M3 plan at .ai/plans/M3-project-registration.md (Status: Awaiting Approval). The first M3 task (T-018) is promoted to Ready. The M2 closeout commit is created on the feature branch and fast-forwarded into main. The m2 annotated milestone tag is created at the M2 closeout commit on main. The feature branch is deleted per the branching strategy. Push is skipped (no explicit authorisation in this session).
M2.6.commit_message: chore(m2.6): close M2 with retrospective, milestone closeout standard, and M3 plan

milestones.name: Project Registration
milestones.primary_outcome: A user can register a project (a folder on disk) and the platform owns a Project entity in the application layer.

M3.1.title: Project Registration Slice 1 (contract + in-memory store + UI surface)
M3.1.summary: M3.1 ships the M3 surface end-to-end as a single slice: the contract (IProjectStore, IProjectService), the in-memory store (InMemoryProjectStore; not durable; the M3 smoke test for the contract; M4-A swaps the IProjectStore registration in AddProjects), the domain entity (Project), the composition root (AddProjects), the UI surface (AppProjectCard, AppProjectList, /projects page), the architecture test (Pages_Resolve_Projects_Through_Service enforces the single-seam rule), 27 new unit tests + 13 new bUnit tests + 2 new architecture tests. Total: 240 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D). 0 warnings, 0 errors. Format clean. Visual smoke: /projects returns 200 on localhost:5286 with the expected markers (1 list slot, 1 empty state, 1 data-state=empty, 1 breadcrumb, 1 page header, 1 /projects in sidebar). The disabled Register button is present in the page header's Actions slot. Three documented deviations: (1) ValidationError is a class, not a struct; (2) IClock is realised through .NET 8+ TimeProvider; (3) Disabled tests unchanged. The M3.1 closeout commit is on main; the M3.1 feature branch is fast-forwarded into main and deleted per the branching strategy. Push is skipped (no remote authorisation).

M3.2.title: Project Registration Slice 2 (form, rename, unregister)
M3.2.summary: M3.2 ships the three mutations the M3 surface exists for end-to-end: the registration modal (RegisterProjectForm.razor; HTML5 native <dialog open>; name + folder path fields; resolves IProjectService through the composition root; calls RegisterAsync; emits OnRegistered on success), the rename modal (RenameProjectForm.razor; pre-fills the project's current name; validates the new name is non-empty + differs from the current name; calls RenameAsync; emits OnRenamed on success), the unregister confirmation (ConfirmUnregisterProject.razor; shows the project name; calls UnregisterAsync; emits OnUnregistered on success). The AppProjectCard Rename + Unregister buttons are enabled; the Open button remains disabled (M4-A's responsibility). The page header's Register a project button is enabled; clicking it opens the registration modal. AppProjectList exposes ShowRegisterDialog() (the page header delegates to it) and RefreshAsync() (the form components invoke it on success). The architecture test Pages_Resolve_Projects_Through_Service is extended with three new tests covering the three new form components; the single-seam rule holds for every form. 30 new bUnit tests (8 RegisterProjectFormTests + 8 RenameProjectFormTests + 5 ConfirmUnregisterProjectTests + 5 AppProjectCardTests + 2 ProjectsPageTests + 5 AppProjectListTests) + 3 new architecture tests added. Total: 273 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D). 0 warnings, 0 errors. Format clean. Visual smoke: /projects returns 200; the Register a project button is enabled; clicking it opens the registration modal; registering / renaming / unregistering a project works end-to-end. Three documented deviations: (1) AppDialog is not introduced; HTML5 native <dialog> is used directly (minimum-blast-radius; the M1.2 design system does not ship a dialog primitive; the design system is not extended); (2) M3.2 unit tests are reused from M3.1 (the M3.1 IProjectServiceTests already cover the RegisterAsync / RenameAsync / UnregisterAsync happy-path + failure paths); (3) Disabled tests are unchanged (the 7 registered-but-disabled tests remain skipped per ADR-016 / M4-D). The M3.2 closeout commit is on main; the M3.2 feature branch is fast-forwarded into main and deleted per the branching strategy. Push is skipped (no remote authorisation).
M3.2.commit_message: feat(m3.2): enable project registration form, rename, and unregister

M3.x.title: M3 closeout — M3 retrospective (per the Milestone Closeout Standard)
M3.x.summary: M3.x (the M3 closeout) ships the M3 retrospective at retrospective-m3-project-registration.md (13 sections per the Milestone Closeout Standard § 4; the second milestone retrospective in the repository), the M4-A plan at .ai/plans/M4-A-infrastructure-process-execution.md (12 sections; Status: Awaiting Approval; the first M4-A task T-021 is Ready), the M3 closeout plan at .ai/plans/M3-closeout.md (mirrors the M2.6 closeout plan's structure), the M3 closeout implementation report at implementation-report-m3-closeout.md (mirrors the M2.6 closeout report's structure), and the M3 closeout per-session handoff at .ai/handoffs/2026-07-11-m3-closeout.md (mirrored to latest.md). M3 is moved from Active to Done with closed_at: 2026-07-11; the m3 annotated milestone tag is at the M3 closeout commit on main per the branching strategy rule 9. The M3 closeout is a docs + workflow + state change; no source code; no M4-A implementation; no provider creation. The project-continuity state is updated per Rule 15: session.json (M3 closeout envelope), tasks.json (T-020 Done; T-021 M4-A.1 Ready; T-007 M3 summary Done), current.md (M3 row Done; next recommended task T-021), task-board.md (M3 closeout in Done Recently; T-021 in Ready; M3 summary archived in Deferred), milestones.json (this file), ROADMAP.md (§ 2 M3 row Done; § 3 M3 closeout row Delivered; M3 DoD bullets checked), .ai/plans/master-delivery-plan.md (§ 1 M3 row Done; § 3 M3 block Done; M3 closeout slice row Delivered). Validation: 273 passed, 0 failed, 7 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects green. Zero deviations: the M3 closeout follows the Milestone Closeout Standard as-is (the standard is mature enough to be reused without modification; the M2.6 closeout's 'introduce the standard' is amortised). Push is skipped (no remote authorisation).
M3.x.commit_message: chore(m3.closeout): close M3 with retrospective, M4-A plan, and m3 milestone tag

milestones.name: Infrastructure / Process Execution
milestones.primary_outcome: AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure is created. IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and the on-disk IProjectStore land. M3 in-memory store is replaced behind the same IProjectStore contract. The Open action on AppProjectCard is enabled in M4-A.2.

M4-A.1.title: Infrastructure project skeleton (IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, on-disk IProjectStore)
M4-A.1.summary: M4-A.1 ships the infrastructure seam every later milestone composes: the new AiEng.Platform.Infrastructure csproj; the four contracts in Application/Infrastructure/ (IProcessRunner + ProcessResult + ICredentialVault + IPlatformInfo); the four implementations in Infrastructure/ (SystemProcessRunner + WindowsCredentialVault + SystemPlatformInfo + JsonFileProjectStore); the AddInfrastructure composition root extension; the one-line swap in AddProjects (the M3 in-memory IProjectStore registration is removed; the on-disk JsonFileProjectStore is now registered through AddInfrastructure). The InMemoryProjectStore is moved to tests/AiEng.Platform.UnitTests/Infrastructure/InMemoryProjectStore.cs as a test fixture. 45 new unit tests across 4 test files (22 JsonFileProjectStoreTests + 11 IProcessRunnerTests + 10 WindowsCredentialVaultTests + 3 SystemPlatformInfoTests) + 2 new architecture tests (Infrastructure_Respects_ProcessBoundary + Infrastructure_Respects_CredentialBoundary; both registered-but-disabled per ADR-016; activate in M4-D). docs/infrastructure.md (10 sections) and docs/projects.md § 4 boundary section update ship. M4-A.1 does NOT enable the Open action on AppProjectCard (the M4-A.2 slice enables the Open action). Total: 318 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects returns 200; the project list persists across an application restart. Three documented deviations: (1) WindowsCredentialVault uses direct P/Invoke over advapi32.dll (no NuGet dependency) per the M4-A plan § 8 risk 4 deferred decision. (2) JsonFileProjectStore uses File.Replace for atomic Windows file replacement. (3) The M4-A.1 ships 45 unit tests (within the M4-A plan's 50+ bound). Branch: feature/T-021-m4-a-1-infrastructure-project-skeleton. Commit: feat(m4-a.1): add infrastructure project skeleton with IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and on-disk IProjectStore. Push is staged for push (not authorised in this session).
M4-A.1.commit_message: feat(m4-a.1): add infrastructure project skeleton with IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and on-disk IProjectStore

M4-A.2.title: Open action on AppProjectCard (IProcessRunner activation; IPlatformInfo.IsWindows extension)
M4-A.2.summary: M4-A.2 ships the Open action on AppProjectCard — the first IProcessRunner activation. IPlatformInfo is extended with bool IsWindows { get; } (implemented in SystemPlatformInfo via RuntimeInformation.IsOSPlatform(OSPlatform.Windows)). AppProjectCard.razor @injects IProcessRunner + IPlatformInfo + ILogger<AppProjectCard> directly (the M4-A plan § 2 item 8 'direct IProcessRunner injection' decision; no IProjectService.OpenAsync facade and no IOpenProjectAction seam). The click handler calls IProcessRunner.RunToCompletionAsync('explorer.exe', new[] { Project.Path }, default). The button is gated on IPlatformInfo.IsWindows (computed Disabled=@(!IsWindowsHost)). Exceptions (Win32Exception + InvalidOperationException + IOException) are swallowed and surfaced as a transient inline OpenError (<div class='app-project-card-open-error' role='alert'>). 5 new bUnit tests (Open_Button_Is_Enabled_When_Host_Is_Windows, Open_Button_Is_Disabled_When_Host_Is_Not_Windows, Clicking_Open_Invokes_IProcessRunner_With_Explorer_And_ProjectPath, Open_Click_Passes_ProjectPath_Single_Element_As_Argument, Open_Click_Swallows_Process_Exceptions) + 1 new active architecture test (AppProjectCard_resolves_open_through_IProcessRunner). The M3.2 Open_Button_Remains_Disabled_In_M3_2 test is deleted. docs/infrastructure.md § 7 is rewritten from future tense to delivered tense; docs/infrastructure.md § 6 documents the new IsWindows property; docs/projects.md § 1/§ 4/§ 5.1/§ 7.2/§ 7.3 are updated; ROADMAP.md and .ai/plans/master-delivery-plan.md are updated; .ai/state/capabilities.json C-012 is updated to Delivered. Total: 323 passed, 0 failed, 9 skipped (per ADR-016 / M4-D); 0 warnings, 0 errors; format clean; visual smoke on /projects returns 200; the Open action is enabled on Windows hosts. M4-A.2 is +5 bUnit + 1 new active architecture vs M4-A.1 closeout (the 2 M4-A.1 architecture tests remain registered-but-disabled per ADR-016). Four documented deviations: (1) IProcessRunner.RunToCompletionAsync takes IReadOnlyList<string> arguments (the M4-A plan § 2 item 8 prose example used a single string with manual quote escaping; the actual contract uses ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList for proper quoting); (2) IPlatformInfo.IsWindows is added in M4-A.2 (the M4-A plan § 8 risk row 6 anticipated this addition); (3) ILogger<AppProjectCard> is introduced in M4-A.2 (the App layer had not previously used ILogger<T>; the BCL provides it via the Blazor Server host DI container; no registration is needed); (4) OpenError inline rendering uses a single scoped CSS class .app-project-card-open-error (no new design-system AppInlineError primitive; the M3.2 minimum-blast-radius decision is preserved). Branch: feature/T-022-m4-a-2-open-action. Commit: feat(m4-a.2): enable AppProjectCard.Open action via IProcessRunner. Push is staged for push (not authorised in this session).
M4-A.2.commit_message: feat(m4-a.2): enable AppProjectCard.Open action via IProcessRunner (hash c9bdb0c)

commits: M4-A.1: feat(m4-a.1): add infrastructure project skeleton with IProcessRunner, ICredentialVault, IPlatformInfo, and on-disk IProjectStore on feature/T-021-m4-a-1-infrastructure-project-skeleton (fast-forwarded into main; branch deleted per the branching strategy rule 7)

milestones.name: Capability Detection
milestones.primary_outcome: The host detects git, ollama, powershell.exe, wsl.exe, wt.exe, bash.exe through IProcessRunner and produces a typed HostCapabilities report; the report is surfaced through the /diagnostics page (AppCapabilityList + AppKeyValueList) and is logged at startup (Information level).

evidence.plan_status: Approved (via Next invocation per .ai/commands.md section 4 + Progressive Coding Rule section 7.1)
evidence.plan_promotion_commit: chore(m4-b.plan): draft M4-B capability detection plan in Awaiting Approval

slices.title: Contract + implementation + composition root + unit tests (IHostCapabilitiesService + HostCapabilities + HostCapability + SystemHostCapabilitiesService + AddHostCapabilities + 20 unit tests + 3 in-line test doubles; the architecture test is deferred to M4-B.3)
slices.commit: feat(m4-b.1): add IHostCapabilitiesService contract and SystemHostCapabilitiesService implementation
deviations: The Capabilities_Resolved_Through_Service architecture test is deferred to M4-B.3 (the test asserts Diagnostics.razor contains @inject IHostCapabilitiesService; Diagnostics.razor does not exist in M4-B.1).

The DetectedAt test was split into two tests: (a) DetectedAt equals the TimeProvider value (deterministic with FixedTimeProvider); (b) DetectedAt falls within the call window when the default clock is used (non-deterministic but bounded).

The Capabilities_Resolved_Through_Service architecture test scopes the forbidden-token check to App/Components/Diagnostics/ (not App/Components/) to avoid the M4-A.2 Open Action false positive on AppProjectCard.razor (which is in App/Components/Projects/, not in App/Components/Diagnostics/).

The docs/capabilities.md 10 sections mirror docs/infrastructure.md 1-10; section 11 (M4-B Consumers) is not added because docs/infrastructure.md already has section 11 from the M4-A.2 handoff (the M4-B.2 handoff section 13 anticipated this; the M4-B.3 closeout confirms the no-scope-creep decision).

The M4-B.3 first session does NOT begin M4-C (provider registry) / M4-D (first concrete process providers) / provider creation (per the brief: Do not create providers + Do not begin the following task). The next session is the M4-B closeout session (T-027) on the user Approve / Next invocation.

deviations: Zero deviations. The M4-B closeout follows the Milestone Closeout Standard as-is (the standard is mature enough to be reused without modification; the M2.6 closeout's "introduce the standard" is amortised). The M4-B closeout mirrors the M3 closeout's structure with M4-B-specific evidence; the M4-B retrospective mirrors the M3 retrospective's structure with M4-B-specific evidence.
slices.next_step: The next session approves the M4-C plan and begins the M4-C.1 implementation (T-028) on the user's Approve or Next invocation. The M4-B closeout does NOT begin M4-C, M4-D, or any provider creation (per the brief: 'Do not begin the following task' and the Progressive Coding Rule).

milestones.name: First Concrete Process Providers
milestones.primary_outcome: GitProvider and OllamaLaunchProvider land as concrete process-boundary providers; the contract test framework is established; the four composition-root architecture tests activate; the No_DirectProcessStart_OutsideInfrastructure test activates.

milestones.name: Native Git Worktrees
milestones.primary_outcome: A user can create a native Git worktree from a registered project; the worktree is the unit of work for later milestones.

milestones.name: Agent Runtime Launching
milestones.primary_outcome: A user can launch an agent runtime against a registered project's worktree; the launch is cancellable mid-stream; the terminal output is rendered; the execution history is persisted.

milestones.name: Review and Quality Gates
milestones.primary_outcome: A launched run can be reviewed (IReviewProvider) and gated (IQualityGateProvider). The native baselines are the first implementations; external tools (Lavish Axi, No Mistakes) are added through the registry when the user opts in.

milestones.name: Autonomous Loops, Orchestration, Production Hardening
milestones.primary_outcome: The platform ships its remaining provider families (IAutonomousLoopProvider, IOrchestrationProvider) through native baselines, and the production-hardening work lands.

milestones.name: AI Session Router (operating layer, one-time)
milestones.primary_outcome: Operating-layer PowerShell 5.1+ supervisor that drives one bounded Claude Code child session per phase, each on a different configured cloud model. Stops at closeout. Does not begin another task. Does not push.
